definitions.text: Himself, herself, itself, themself, themselves; that specific (person mentioned).
examples: This argument was put forward by the defendant self.
I made out a cheque, payable to self, which cheered me up somewhat.

definitions.text: One individual's personality, character, demeanor, or disposition.
examples: She remained her usual cheerful self despite recent setbacks

definitions.text: The subject of one's own experience of phenomena: perception, emotions, thoughts.
An individual person as the object of the person's own reflective consciousness (plural selves).
To fertilize by the same individual; to self-fertilize or self-pollinate.
To fertilize by the same strain; to inbreed.
Having its own or a single nature or character throughout, as in colour, composition, etc., without addition or change; of the same kind; unmixed.
examples: a self bow: one made from a single piece of wood
a self flower or plant: one which is wholly of one colour
